2606.12386.title: ATLAS: Active Theory Learning for Automated Science
2606.12386.paper_summary: Advancing scientific understanding through mechanistic modeling requires posing the right experimental questions to yield maximally informative data. To automate this pursuit within cognitive science, we introduce ATLAS (Active Theory Learning for Automated Science), an active learning framework for the data-driven discovery of interpretable behavioral models. ATLAS iterates between generating mechanistic hypotheses--instantiated as a diverse ensemble of sparse neural networks (Disentangled RNNs)--and designing experiments that optimally distinguish between them. We test this approach on the problem of recovering reinforcement learning agents from their behavior in bandit tasks. ATLAS designs varied sequences of qualitatively novel experiments with temporal structure tailored to underlying agent characteristics. The models trained on these experiments are evaluated against a comprehensive set of metrics for mechanistic modeling that capture behavioral, structural, and computational similarity. ATLAS achieves a 5-10x improvement in sample efficiency across all metrics compared to random experimentation, and its performance is further validated against expert-designed experiments derived from literature. These in silico results showcase ATLAS's potential to accelerate human-interpretable insights in cognitive science and other domains where scientific inquiry relies on discovering mechanistic models.
2606.12386.paper_summary_zh: 透過機制建模推進科學理解需要提出正確的實驗問題，以產生最大的信息量數據。為了在認知科學中自動化這一追求，我們引入了ATLAS（自動科學的主動理論學習），這是一個用於數據驅動的可解釋行為模型發現的主動學習框架。ATLAS在生成機制假設（具體體現為多樣的稀疏神經網絡集群，即解耦RNN）和設計最佳區分這些假設的實驗之間進行迭代。我們在從行為中恢復強化學習代理的問題上測試這種方法，這些行為來自於賭徒任務。ATLAS設計了多樣的質量新穎實驗序列，這些實驗具有針對基礎代理特徵的時間結構。對這些實驗訓練的模型進行了全面的機制建模指標評估，這些指標捕捉行為、結構和計算相似性。與隨機實驗相比，ATLAS在所有指標上實現了5-10倍的樣本效率提升，其性能進一步通過來自文獻的專家設計實驗進行驗證。這些計算結果展示了ATLAS在加速認知科學及其他依賴於發現機制模型的科學探究領域中獲得人類可解釋見解的潛力。
2606.12386.author: Noémi Éltető et.al.
2606.12386.authors: Noémi Éltető, Nathaniel D. Daw, Kimberly L. Stachenfeld, Kevin J. Miller
